backend/core/analytics/signal_evaluator.py:54-63 — SignalRegistry._load() dict keyed by signal_id. Lines 54-60: 'if signal_id in signals_by_id: existing_source = str(signals_by_id[signal_id].get(_source_path, "")); if str(path) <= existing_source: continue' — deterministic lexicographic overwrite policy. No error, no multi-frame retention. SignalResult emitted once per signal_id maximum.

backend/core/models/signal.py:10-20 — SignalResult Pydantic model has fields: signal_id, system, signal_state, signal_value, confidence, confidence_reasons, primary_metric, lab_normal_but_flagged, supporting_markers, explanation. Fields activation_key, spec_id, package_id, source_document are ALL ABSENT. ADR-RT-002 Decision 5 (line 59-68) requires all four fields on SignalResult.

Optional fields (activation_keys_emitted, collisions_detected, policy_version) may remain optional while DRAFT. Carry-forward intact.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:189-211 — scope items 1-11 define exactly: replace silent collapse; introduce activation_key; preserve signal_id; update SignalRegistry; prevent duplicate activation_key collisions; update SignalEvaluator for multi-frame; update SignalResult provenance; add regression tests; downstream impact report. Scope is bounded and not open-ended.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:300-313 — 7 test categories listed: (1) existing SignalRegistry/SignalEvaluator tests; (2) new regression for no silent collapse; (3) new regression for duplicate activation_key fails closed; (4) ALT high pilot multi-frame test; (5) existing single-frame tests; (6) root-cause/interaction/IDL tests if payload shape affected; (7) golden fixture tests if output structure changed. Existing test file: backend/tests/unit/test_signal_evaluator.py confirmed on disk.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:330 — STOP #10: 'Deterministic ordering of evaluated frames cannot be guaranteed.' Sprint STOP conditions protect against non-determinism. Cursor must guarantee frame ordering is deterministic (not just path-dependent after registry key change).

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:316-332 — 12 STOP conditions. Explicit STOPs for: compile manifest schema changes (#8), ARCH-RT-1 carry-forward violation (#9), package regeneration (#5), runtime identity requiring package file modification (#6), scope expansion (#12). Coverage is comprehensive.

ADR-RT-002:38-40 requires activation_key format signal_id::spec_id. pkg_s24_alt_high_hepatocellular_injury/signal_library.yaml contains NO spec_id or activation_key fields. Sprint scope item 2 says 'Introduce or wire activation_key as runtime activation-frame identity'. Sprint out-of-scope explicitly prohibits modifying package files. STOP #6 covers this: 'Runtime identity cannot be changed without modifying package files.' Cursor MUST evaluate at authority preflight whether activation_key can be derived at runtime (e.g., from package_id or directory name) or via sidecar index WITHOUT touching package YAMLs. If STOP #6 fires, implementation must halt and report.

backend/core/models/signal.py:10 — SignalResult is consumed by downstream pipeline. Adding activation_key, spec_id, package_id fields changes the model contract. evaluate_all() at signal_evaluator.py:386 currently returns List[SignalResult] with one entry per signal_id. After sprint, multiple entries may share signal_id (different activation_key). Any downstream code that deduplicates by signal_id will silently collapse frames again. Cursor must identify all SignalResult consumers and confirm no post-evaluation deduplication by signal_id exists.

backend/core/analytics/signal_evaluator.py:468 — 'results.sort(key=lambda r: r.signal_id)'. After sprint, multiple SignalResult rows may share signal_id (distinct activation_key). Sort by signal_id alone is non-deterministic for same-signal_id frames. Sort key must become (signal_id, activation_key) or equivalent to guarantee deterministic frame ordering. STOP #10 requires this be addressed.

docs/architecture/signal_id_collision_inventory.md:120-122 — 'Per-package spec_id inside batch JSON was not denormalised into this table; frame identity is inferred from package directory suffix and PSI investigation_spec_id (kb47 only).' Cursor must verify whether kb52c signal_library.yaml files contain spec_id fields that can be used as activation_key components, or whether only package directory suffix is available. This determines activation_key derivation strategy.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:274-299 — out-of-scope list prohibits: package files; investigation specs; PSI artefacts; root-cause YAML; health systems card evidence; compiled hypothesis artefacts; PSI runtime wiring; activation compiler; compile manifest schema/validator (unless unavoidable with STOP first); frontend; IDL; biomarker SSOT; reference ranges; scoring rails; clinical thresholds; fallback parsers; helper scripts. All STOP conditions also protect against scope drift.

ADR-RT-002 is the sole identity policy authority. ARCH-RT-1 contracts (compile_manifest_contract.md, activation_compile_contract.md, package_provenance_policy.md, activation_compile_contract.md) are complementary, not competing. No duplicate authority for signal identity policy detected.
